title: pilosella densiflora subsp. suebophila
intro: Pilosella densiflora subsp. suebophila, also known as a subspecies of the Alpine Hawk's-beard, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Pilosella densiflora subsp. suebophila include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
